pattern: Because an in-order walk of a BST yields sorted values, the smallest difference must come from a pair of consecutive values in that sorted sequence.
hints: Visiting a BST in-order produces its values in increasing order.
Once values are sorted, the closest pair is always adjacent in that order.
Track the previously visited value during the in-order walk and keep the minimum gap between successive values.

edge_inputs.description: two nodes give a single difference
balanced BST where adjacent leaves are closest
smallest gap spans across subtrees, not within one
minimum difference is between a parent and a deep descendant
left-skewed BST with tight consecutive values

stress.description: random BST of distinct values serialized level-order
stress.note: distinct values guarantee at least two nodes and a valid BST

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def getMinimumDifference(self, root):
        vals = []
        stack = [root]
        while stack:
            node = stack.pop()
            if node is None:
                continue
            vals.append(node.val)
            stack.append(node.left)
            stack.append(node.right)
        vals.sort()
        best = min(vals[i + 1] - vals[i] for i in range(len(vals) - 1))
        return best
